ItacPersonItem.Notes: At the service of Cardinal Spada in Rome, and Marquis Villa in Turin. In 1556, in Rome, at a gathering of Cristina of Sweden`s Academy in Farnese palace, Melosio recited `La bugia`, an improvised poem inspired by a silver candle bearer, which he won in a lottery. The poem was based on the double meaning of the word `bugia`. See Melosio`s biography by Marco Catucci, in DBI.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
